system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

}

thermoType
{

mixture

    thermodynamics

        Hf          0;
    }

boundaryField

boundaryField
{

    {

    top
    {

        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;

    {
        type            slip;

        type            empty;
    }
}

vertices

    (  0.3048   0.005    0.005 )   // 13
    (  0.3048   0.1524    0.005 )   // 14
    ( -0.15242  0.1524    0.005 )   // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Lower region (0-3, 4-7)
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper region (8-11, 12-15)
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (40 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (8 12 15 11)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (9 10 14 13)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (8 9 13 12)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (8 11 10 9)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (11 15 14 10)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoCentralFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 

    patch type 'patch' not constraint type 'empty'
    for patch frontAndBack of field T in file "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/experiment-finetuned-allrun_commands/single_knockout/knockout_allrun_commands/runs/wedge/3/0/T"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/experiment-finetuned-allrun_commands/single_knockout/knockout_allrun_commands/runs/wedge/3/0/T/boundaryField/frontAndBack from line 44 to line 44.

    From function Foam::emptyFvPatchField<Type>::emptyFvPatchField(const Foam::fvPatch&, const Foam::DimensionedField<Type, Foam::volMesh>&, const Foam::dictionary&) [with Type = double]
    in file fields/fvPatchFields/constraint/empty/emptyFvPatchField.C at line 54.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — patch type 'patch' not constraint type 'empty' for frontAndBack of field T

This error is clear and straightforward. The `frontAndBack` patch is defined as `type patch;` in the `blockMeshDict`, but the `T` field's boundary condition file expects it to be of type `empty`. The `empty` constraint type is used for periodic or symmetry boundaries where no flux crosses the boundary, and it's a stricter constraint than `patch`.

### Root Cause

In `blockMeshDict`, the `frontAndBack` patch is defined as:

```
frontAndBack
{
    type patch;
    faces
    (
        (0 3 2 1)
        (8 11 10 9)
        (3 7 6 2)
        (11 15 14 10)
    );
}
```

But in the `T` boundary condition file (`0/T`), the `frontAndBack` entry specifies:

```
frontAndBack
{
    type            empty;
}
```

The `rhoCentralFoam` solver requires that any patch constrained as `empty` in a field's boundary condition must also be declared as `type empty;` in the mesh's `blockMeshDict`. Currently, it's declared as `type patch;`, which causes the mismatch.

### Proposed Fix

**In `blockMeshDict`, change the `frontAndBack` patch type from `patch` to `empty`:**

```
frontAndBack
{
    type            empty;
    faces
    (
        (0 3 2 1)
        (8 11 10 9)
        (3 7 6 2)
        (11 15 14 10)
    );
}
```

This makes the mesh patch declaration consistent with the boundary condition constraint used by the `T` field. The `empty` type is appropriate for this patch since it represents the internal faces at y=0 and y=0.1524 that connect the two blocks and should have no flux across them (consistent with the `empty` boundary condition in `0/T`).

## Error: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cannot find "points"

This is a **consequence** of the `blockMesh` failure. Once `blockMesh` runs successfully with the above fix, this error will resolve automatically.

## Summary

The only required change is in `blockMeshDict`: change the `frontAndBack` patch type from `patch` to `empty` to match the `empty` constraint used in the `T` boundary condition file. This is a simple one-line change.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change frontAndBack patch type from patch to empty; Keep all other patches and block definitions unchanged'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 2 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
